findings.message: 'significant work' has no measurable threshold; the hook cannot reliably distinguish significant from insignificant work.
'genuinely blocked' is undefined; no criteria distinguish genuine blockage from ordinary uncertainty.
'Trivial tasks' is vague; only one example given ('reading files'), leaving the boundary ambiguous.

'prestart' script runs 'bun install --silent' on every server start, fetching remote packages from the registry each invocation; combined with semver ranges, the installed dependency set can change between starts.
@ngrok/ngrok opens an outbound public internet tunnel on every server start; intentional for Twilio webhook delivery but means every run exposes a new public endpoint.
'bun-types' is pinned to 'latest' with no semver range; resolves to whatever version is current at install time.
Four runtime dependencies (@modelcontextprotocol/sdk, @ngrok/ngrok, openai, ws) use '^' ranges; minor/patch upgrades are pulled automatically on each bun install.
SKILL.md has no YAML frontmatter block; the required 'name' field is absent, preventing reliable skill indexing and cross-linking.
SKILL.md has no YAML frontmatter block; the required 'description' field is absent, preventing trigger-phrase resolution and /help surfacing.
'significant task' has no size or scope criterion; any task could be argued significant by the model.
'complex decisions' is undefined; no criteria for what makes a decision complex enough to warrant a phone call.
